原文:

tom hollon

Posted on Jul 8

In recent years, the world of digital art and collectibles has been revolutionized by the emergence of Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs). NFTs are unique digital assets that are stored on a blockchain, providing verifiable ownership and scarcity. One of the key components driving the NFT ecosystem is the NFT marketplace, a platform where buyers and sellers can trade these digital assets. This article serves as the ultimate guide to NFT marketplace development, providing insights into the process and key considerations.

What is an NFT Marketplace?

An NFT marketplace is an online platform that facilitates the buying, selling, and trading of non-fungible tokens. It acts as an intermediary between creators, collectors, and investors, providing a secure and efficient environment for NFT transactions. NFT marketplaces offer features such as browsing and searching for NFTs, auctioning, bidding, and setting prices. They enable artists and content creators to showcase and sell their unique digital creations while allowing collectors to discover and purchase exclusive items.

Benefits of NFT Marketplace Development

NFT marketplace development offers numerous benefits for creators, collectors, and investors alike. Some of the key advantages include:

  • Global Reach: NFT marketplaces provide access to a global audience, allowing creators to reach a larger customer base and collectors to explore a wide range of unique digital assets from around the world.
  • Transparency and Authenticity: NFTs are built on blockchain technology, ensuring transparency and immutability of ownership. This enhances trust among participants, as the history and provenance of each NFT can be easily verified.
  • Fair Royalties and Secondary Sales: Smart contracts embedded in NFTs enable creators to receive royalties whenever their assets are resold in the secondary market. This provides ongoing revenue streams and incentives for artists.
  • Fractional Ownership and Investment: NFTs can be divided into fractions, allowing multiple investors to own a share of a valuable digital asset. This fractional ownership model enables broader participation and liquidity.
  • Censorship Resistance: NFTs and their transactions are recorded on a decentralized blockchain, making them resistant to censorship and ensuring the freedom of expression for artists and collectors.

To create a successful NFT marketplace, certain key features need to be incorporated. These include:

  • User-friendly Interface: An intuitive and user-friendly interface is crucial for attracting and retaining users. It should provide easy navigation, search filters, and sorting options for a seamless browsing experience.
  • Secure Wallet Integration: Integration with secure digital wallets is essential for users to manage their NFTs and conduct transactions securely. Compatibility with popular wallets like MetaMask and Trust Wallet is recommended.
  • Smart Contract Integration: Smart contracts play a vital role in NFT marketplaces, facilitating ownership, transfers, and royalties. Integrating a robust and audited smart contract system ensures trust and security.
  • Auction and Bidding Mechanism: NFT marketplaces often include auction and bidding features, allowing users to bid on or auction their NFTs. This feature creates excitement and encourages engagement within the community.
  • Community and Social Features: Building a vibrant community around an NFT marketplace is crucial for its success. Social features such as user profiles, comments, likes, and sharing options help foster interaction and engagement.

Steps to Develop an NFT Marketplace

Creating an NFT marketplace involves several essential steps. Here's a step-by-step guide to developing an NFT marketplace:

Conduct thorough research to understand the market, target audience, and competition. Define your goals and objectives, identify unique selling points, and outline the scope of your NFT marketplace project.

  1. Design and User Experience

Create a visually appealing and user-friendly design for your NFT marketplace. Consider the overall user experience, ensuring seamless navigation, clear call-to-actions, and attractive visuals to showcase the NFTs effectively.

  1. Smart Contract Development

Develop and audit the smart contracts that power the NFT marketplace. Smart contracts handle the creation, ownership, and transfer of NFTs, as well as the implementation of royalty mechanisms and other features.

  1. Front-End and Back-End Development

Implement the front-end and back-end functionalities of the NFT marketplace. This includes building the user interface, integrating the smart contracts, developing secure wallet integrations, and creating APIs for seamless data exchange.

Thoroughly test the NFT marketplace to identify and fix any bugs or vulnerabilities. Perform security audits to ensure the protection of user data, wallets, and transactions. Implement necessary security measures such as encryption and two-factor authentication.

  1. Deployment and Maintenance

Deploy the NFT marketplace in a secure and scalable hosting environment. Regularly monitor and maintain the platform, addressing any performance issues, adding new features, and keeping up with security updates.

Choosing the Right NFT Marketplace Development Company

When embarking on an NFT marketplace development project, choosing the right development company is crucial. Consider the following factors when selecting an NFT marketplace development company:

  • Experience and Expertise: Look for a development company with experience in blockchain, smart contract development, and NFT marketplace projects. They should have a deep understanding of the latest trends and technologies.
  • Portfolio and Track Record: Review their portfolio to assess the quality of their previous work. Check for successful NFT marketplace projects they have delivered and client testimonials.
  • Technology Stack: Ensure that the company uses modern and secure technologies in their development process. They should be well-versed in blockchain frameworks, front-end and back-end technologies, and security best practices.
  • Communication and Collaboration: Effective communication and collaboration are essential for a smooth development process. Choose a company that maintains transparent communication channels and keeps you involved at each stage of development.
  • Post-Development Support: NFT marketplace development is an ongoing process. Confirm that the development company offers post-development support, including maintenance, updates, and assistance with scalability and future enhancements.

NFT Marketplace Development Solutions

Several NFT marketplace development solutions are available in the market. These solutions provide ready-to-use frameworks and platforms that can be customized according tospecific requirements. Some popular NFT marketplace development solutions include:

  • OpenSea: OpenSea is one of the largest and most well-known NFT marketplaces. They offer a development platform that allows customization and integration with existing projects.
  • Rarible: Rarible provides an open marketplace protocol for creating customizable NFT marketplaces. Developers can leverage their smart contract templates and API to build their own marketplace.
  • SuperRare: SuperRare is a curated NFT marketplace focused on digital art. They offer an API and developer tools for building applications and integrating with their platform.
  • Mintable: Mintable provides a user-friendly platform for creating, selling, and trading NFTs. They offer customizable templates and tools for developers to build their own NFT marketplaces.
  • Axie Infinity: Axie Infinity is a blockchain-based game that also serves as an NFT marketplace. Developers can explore their developer resources and APIs to create unique gaming experiences and integrate with the marketplace.

Future Trends in NFT Marketplaces

NFT marketplaces are constantly evolving, and several trends are shaping their future. Some key trends to watch out for include:

  • Cross-Chain Compatibility: As blockchain technology advances, NFT marketplaces are exploring cross-chain compatibility, allowing NFTs to be traded across different blockchain networks.
  • Enhanced Interactivity: NFT marketplaces are incorporating more interactive features, such as virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR), to provide immersive experiences for collectors and users.
  • Social Token Integration: The integration of social tokens with NFT marketplaces is gaining traction. Social tokens allow creators to build communities and offer unique benefits to their supporters.
  • Environmental Sustainability: With the increasing concern about the carbon footprint of blockchain networks, NFT marketplaces are exploring more eco-friendly solutions, such as the use of energy-efficient blockchains or carbon offset programs.
  • Metaverse Integration: NFT marketplaces are moving towards integration with metaverse platforms, enabling users to showcase and interact with their NFT collections in virtual worlds.

Conclusion

NFT marketplace development has opened up new opportunities for artists, collectors, and investors in the digital realm. Building a successful NFT marketplace requires careful planning, smart contract development, user-friendly design, and robust security measures. By understanding the key features, development process, and future trends in NFT marketplaces, you can embark on your journey to create a thriving platform in the exciting world of non-fungible tokens.

  • 它提供了与本机 C++ 库的快速、直接绑定，可通过简单的 JS API 访问这些库。

  1. 获取和设置字符串、布尔值和数字
  2. 完全同步调用，没有异步/等待，没有 Promises，没有 Bridge。
  3. 加密支持（安全存储）
  4. 多实例支持（将用户数据与全局数据分开）
  5. 自定义存储位置
  6. 高性能，因为一切都是用 C++ 编写的
  7. 比 AsyncStorage 快约 30 倍
  8. 使用 JSI 而不是“旧”桥
  9. 易于使用的 React Hooks API
  10. 无异步/等待


单击此处比较流行的 React Native 存储解决方案。

安装

yarn add react-native-mmkv && cd ios && pod install

或者

npm install react-native-mmkv && cd ios && pod install

用法

创建一个新实例

建议您在整个应用程序中重复使用此实例，而不是每次都创建一个新实例，因此请导出存储对象。

 import { MMKV } from 'react-native-mmkv' export const storage = new MMKV()

或者

import { MMKV } from 'react-native-mmkv' export const storageEncrypted = new MMKV({ id: `user-${userId}-storage`, //required: if when either path/encryptionKey exist path: `${USER_DIRECTORY}/storage`, //optional: changing storage path encryptionKey: 'hunter2' //optional: storing all values encrypted })

原文:

Ajmal Hasan

Posted on Jul 8

  • The fastest key/value storage for React Native.

  • MMKV is an efficient, small mobile key-value storage framework developed by WeChat.

  • react-native-mmkv is a library that allows you to easily use MMKV inside your React Native applications. It provides fast and direct bindings to the native C++ library which are accessible through a simple JS API.

  1. Get and set strings, booleans and numbers
  2. Fully synchronous calls, no async/await, no Promises, no Bridge.
  3. Encryption support (secure storage)
  4. Multiple instances support (separate user-data with global data)
  5. Customize storage location
  6. High performance because everything is written in C++
  7. ~30x faster than AsyncStorage
  8. Uses JSI instead of the "old" Bridge
  9. iOS, Android and Web support
  10. Easy to use React Hooks API
  11. No Async/Await


Click here to compare popular storage solutions for React Native.

Installation 

yarn add react-native-mmkv && cd ios && pod install

or

npm install react-native-mmkv && cd ios && pod install

Usage

Create a new instance

It is recommended that you re-use this instance throughout your entire app instead of creating a new instance each time, so export the storage object.

import { MMKV } from 'react-native-mmkv'
export const storage = new MMKV()

or

import { MMKV } from 'react-native-mmkv'
export const storageEncrypted = new MMKV({
  id: `user-${userId}-storage`, //required: if when either path/encryptionKey exist
  path: `${USER_DIRECTORY}/storage`, //optional: changing storage path
  encryptionKey: 'hunter2' //optional: storing all values encrypted
})

Click here to check how to use it in app

Click here to see how to use it as an hook like useState

Click here to add listener when ever some key changes and you want to perform some action on that

CLick here to see how to integrate MMKV with redux-persist

Click here to use reactotron-react-native-mmkv to automatically log writes to your MMKV storage using Reactotron.

Integration of mmkv with redux persist and reactotron:

constants.js

import { MMKV } from 'react-native-mmkv'

export const storage = new MMKV(
    //     {
    //     id: `user-custom-storage`,
    //     //   path: `${USER_DIRECTORY}/storage`,
    //     encryptionKey: 'hunter2'
    // }
)

export const reduxStorageMMKV = {
    setItem: (key, value) => {
        storage.set(key, value)
        return Promise.resolve(true)
    },
    getItem: (key) => {
        const value = storage.getString(key)
        return Promise.resolve(value)
    },
    removeItem: (key) => {
        storage.delete(key)
        return Promise.resolve()
    },
}

store.js

import { combineReducers, configureStore } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';
import cakeReducer from './slices/cakeSlice';
import icecreamReducer from './slices/icecreamSlice';
import userReducer from './slices/userSlice';
import Reactotron from './reactotronConfig/ReactotronConfig';
import {
  persistReducer,
  FLUSH,
  REHYDRATE,
  PAUSE,
  PERSIST,
  PURGE,
  REGISTER,
} from 'redux-persist';
// import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import { reduxStorageMMKV } from '../constants/constants';
// import logger from 'redux-logger';

const persistConfig = {
  key: 'root',
  version: 1,
  storage: reduxStorageMMKV, //<-- put here 
  blacklist: ['icecream', 'cake'], //these reduce will not persist data
  // whitelist: ['users'], //these reduce will persist data
};
const reducer = combineReducers({
  cake: cakeReducer,
  icecream: icecreamReducer,
  users: userReducer,
});
const persistedReducer = persistReducer(persistConfig, reducer);

//https://redux-toolkit.js.org/usage/usage-guide#use-with-redux-persist
const store = configureStore({
  reducer: persistedReducer,
  middleware: getDefaultMiddleware =>
    getDefaultMiddleware({
      serializableCheck: {
        ignoredActions: [FLUSH, REHYDRATE, PAUSE, PERSIST, PURGE, REGISTER], //
      },
    }),
  // middleware: getDefaultMiddleware => getDefaultMiddleware().concat(logger),
  enhancers: [Reactotron.createEnhancer()],
  devTools: __DEV__,
});

export default store;

ReactotronConfig.js

import Reactotron, { asyncStorage } from 'reactotron-react-native';
import { reactotronRedux } from 'reactotron-redux';
import mmkvPlugin from "reactotron-react-native-mmkv"
import { storage } from '../../constants/constants'

Reactotron.configure({ name: 'app_name' })
  .useReactNative()
  .use(reactotronRedux()) //  <- here i am!
  .use(mmkvPlugin({
    storage, //mmkv instance
    ignore: ["secret", "persist:root"],
    //"persist:root" key will avoid showing redux persist update everytime redux persist 
    //mmkvPlugin() accepts an object with an ignore key. The value is an array of strings you would like to prevent sending to Reactotron.
  }))
  .connect();

const yeOldeConsoleLog = console.log;
// make a new one
console.log = (...args) => {
  // always call the old one, because React Native does magic swizzling too
  yeOldeConsoleLog(...args);
  // send this off to Reactotron.
  Reactotron.display({
    name: 'CONSOLE',
    value: args,
    preview: args.length > 0 && typeof args[0] === 'string' ? args[0] : null,
  });
};

export default Reactotron;

App.js

// /**
//  * Sample React Native App
//  * https://github.com/facebook/react-native
//  *
//  * @format
//  * @flow strict-local
//  */

import React from 'react';
import { Provider } from 'react-redux';
import { PersistGate } from 'redux-persist/integration/react';
import { persistStore } from 'redux-persist';
// import store from './redux/store';
import rtkStore from './reduxToolkit/store';
import HomeScreen from './screens/home';

let persistor = persistStore(rtkStore);

const App = () => {
  return (
    <Provider store={rtkStore}>
      <PersistGate loading={null} persistor={persistor}>
        <HomeScreen />
      </PersistGate>
    </Provider>
  );
};
export default App;

Items.Title: 写一封好的求职信：开发人员指南 | Writing a Good Cover Letter: A Guide for Developers
Items.Description:
精心制作的求职信是开发人员寻找工作机会的重要工具。 它提供了一个展示他们的技能、经验和对该领域的热情的平台。

直接跳到该工具

以下是撰写引人注目的求职信的一些关键技巧：

了解目的：求职信作为介绍，强调您的相关资格并表达您对特定工作或公司的兴趣。 它通过提供额外的背景信息并展示您的个性来补充您的简历。

根据工作量身定制：定制每封求职信以符合工作要求和公司文化。 花时间研究公司并了解他们的价值观、项目和他们使用的技术。 突出显示与职位描述相匹配的相关技能和经验。

强有力的开头：以吸引读者注意力的迷人开头开始。 考虑提及具体的成就、项目或个人关系，以展示您的热情并使自己与其他申请者区分开来。

展示您的技能：清楚地传达您与该职位相关的技术技能和经验。 突出显示您使用过的关键项目、技术和编程语言。 使用具体例子来说明您的成就和贡献。

展示热情：展示您对编码、开发以及整个行业的热情。 讨论任何个人项目、开源贡献或参与技术社区，以证明您的奉献精神和持续学习。

保持简洁：求职信应该简洁明了。 避免冗长的段落，并专注于有效地传达您的资格。 使用要点或短段落来突出关键点，让读者更容易浏览。

校对和编辑：始终校对求职信中的语法、拼写和格式错误。 确保其结构良好且无错误。 请值得信赖的朋友或同事对其进行审核，以获得宝贵的反馈。

为什么它对开发人员很重要？

求职信对开发人员至关重要，原因如下：

展示技能和经验：求职信允许开发人员提供简历中提到的更多有关其技术技能、经验和项目的背景信息。 它可以帮助雇主了解他们的专业知识如何与工作要求相一致。

展示热情和热情：开发人员可以使用求职信来表达他们对编码的热情以及他们感兴趣的特定领域。 这表明了奉献精神、积极性以及对职位和公司的真正兴趣。

个性化申请：一封根据职位描述和公司量身定制的精心撰写的求职信表明开发人员已经完成了研究并且对该机会真正感兴趣。 这有助于他们从其他可能只提交通用申请的申请人中脱颖而出。

强调文化契合度：求职信提供了一个展示与公司文化、价值观和使命的一致性的机会。 开发人员可以突出他们的协作性质、适应能力和解决问题的能力，这些在科技行业中受到高度重视。

结论：
写一封好的求职信对于开发人员来说至关重要，因为它可以让他们以个性化且引人注目的方式展示自己的技能、经验和热情。 通过根据工作要求定制每封求职信并表现出对行业的热情，开发人员可以增加脱颖而出并获得所需工作的机会。 利用CoverLetterSimple.ai等工具可以帮助创建定制且有影响力的求职信，以吸引雇主的注意力并提升他们的工作申请。

A well-crafted cover letter is an essential tool for developers seeking job opportunities. It provides a platform to showcase their skills, experiences, and passion for the field.

JUMP STRAIGHT TO THE TOOL

Here are some key tips to write a compelling cover letter:

Understand the Purpose: A cover letter serves as an introduction, highlighting your relevant qualifications and expressing your interest in a specific job or company. It complements your resume by providing additional context and showcasing your personality.

Tailor it to the Job: Customize each cover letter to align with the job requirements and company culture. Take the time to research the company and understand their values, projects, and technologies they use. Highlight relevant skills and experiences that match the job description.

Start Strong: Begin with a captivating opening that grabs the reader's attention. Consider mentioning a specific achievement, project, or personal connection to demonstrate your enthusiasm and differentiate yourself from other applicants.

Showcase Your Skills: Clearly communicate your technical skills and experiences that are relevant to the position. Highlight key projects, technologies, and programming languages you have worked with. Use specific examples to illustrate your achievements and contributions.

Demonstrate Passion: Show your passion for coding, development, and the industry as a whole. Discuss any personal projects, open-source contributions, or involvement in tech communities that demonstrate your dedication and continuous learning.

Keep it Concise: Cover letters should be concise and to the point. Avoid lengthy paragraphs and focus on conveying your qualifications effectively. Use bullet points or short paragraphs to highlight key points and make it easier for the reader to scan.

Proofread and Edit: Always proofread your cover letter for grammar, spelling, and formatting errors. Ensure that it is well-structured and error-free. Ask a trusted friend or colleague to review it as well to get valuable feedback.

Why is it important for developers?

Cover letters are crucial for developers for several reasons:

Showcasing Skills and Experience: A cover letter allows developers to provide more context about their technical skills, experiences, and projects beyond what is mentioned in their resume. It helps employers understand how their expertise aligns with the job requirements.

Demonstrating Passion and Enthusiasm: Developers can use cover letters to convey their passion for coding and their specific field of interest. This demonstrates dedication, motivation, and a genuine interest in the role and company.

Personalizing the Application: A well-written cover letter tailored to the job description and company shows that developers have done their research and are genuinely interested in the opportunity. It helps them stand out from other applicants who may only submit a generic application.

Highlighting Cultural Fit: Cover letters provide an opportunity to showcase alignment with the company's culture, values, and mission. Developers can highlight their collaborative nature, adaptability, and problem-solving skills, which are highly valued in the tech industry.

Conclusion:
Writing a good cover letter is essential for developers as it allows them to present their skills, experiences, and passion in a personalised and compelling way. By tailoring each cover letter to the job requirements and demonstrating enthusiasm for the industry, developers can increase their chances of standing out and securing their desired job. Leveraging tools like CoverLetterSimple.ai can aid in creating tailored and impactful cover letters that grab employers' attention and elevate their job application.

Items.Title: 使用 AWS Database Migration Service 将 MySQL 迁移到 PostgreSQL | Migrating MySQL to PostgreSQL With the AWS Database Migration Service
Items.Description:
克鲁什·瓦萨尼

发表于 7 月 8 日

迁移数据时，源数据库和目标数据库可以使用相同的数据库引擎，也可以使用不同的引擎。 次要用例是频繁或长期的复制任务。 使用 DMS，可以避免以前涉及中断或有风险的实例突然切换的迁移过程。 相反，跨不同数据库实例设置实时复制允许迁移活动以更小的步骤更慢地发生，并且在每个阶段执行验证。

此外，DMS还可以用于无限期的备份任务。 这通常比传统的数据库备份策略（例如定期快照）成本更高，但当数据量非常大或需要实时备份时，DMS 通常是最有效和高效的解决方案。

在本博客中，我们将使用 DMS 将数据从运行 MySQL 引擎的数据库实例迁移到运行 Aurora PostgreSQL 引擎的实例。

学习目标
这是一个初学者级别的博客，完成此博客后，您将能够：

  1. 在 DMS 中配置源端点和目标端点
  2. 在 DMS 中运行迁移任务
  3. 从命令行连接到 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 数据库
  4. 在 DMS 中生成迁移前任务评估。

先决条件
您应该对以下内容有一个概念性的理解：

  • 亚马逊RDS
  • 脚步：

    1. 在 AWS 管理控制台搜索栏中，输入 Database Migration Service，然后单击 Services 下的 Database Migration Service 结果

    2. 在左侧菜单中，单击端点。

    3. 单击创建端点。

    1. 在“创建端点”表单的“端点类型”部分中，确保选择“源端点”：

    1. 勾选选择RDS数据库实例复选框，并在出现的下拉框中选择mysqlsource：（已经提前创建了该数据库）

    这将使用服务器名称、端口和用户名的值填充表单的端点配置部分。

    1. 在表单的端点配置部分中，选择手动提供访问信息。

    1. 在密码文本框中，输入 testpass。

    通过在此处输入密码，您将明确授予数据库迁移服务对源数据库的访问权限。

    1. 在表单的测试终端节点连接部分的 VPC 下拉菜单中，选择 VPC 测试。 在复制实例下拉列表中，确保选择 lab-replication-instance：

    2. 要创建源端点，请单击创建端点：

    3. 在端点列表中，选择 mysqlsource 端点：

    4. 要打开“测试端点连接”表单，请单击“操作”，然后单击“测试连接”：

    5. 要测试源端点连接，请单击运行测试：

    测试最多需要一分钟才能完成。

    完成后，您将看到状态字段更改为成功：

    如果您没有看到成功的连接，则端点中存储的密码可能不正确。 使用管理控制台修改并重新输入密码。

    使用 EC2 Instance Connect 连接到虚拟机

    1. 在 AWS 管理控制台搜索栏中，输入 EC2，然后单击服务下的 EC2 结果：

    2. 要查看可用实例，请单击左侧菜单中的实例：


    填充源数据库

    脚步

    1. 在 AWS 管理控制台搜索栏中，输入 RDS，然后单击服务下的 RDS 结果：

    1. 在左侧菜单中，单击数据库：

    2. 在数据库列表中，选择 mysqlsource：

    1. 在连接和安全标题下，记下端点，它将类似于以下内容：

    稍后您将使用此端点从 Linux 主机连接到数据库。

    为了避免混淆，DMS 中的端点资源与 RDS 中的端点不同：

    在 RDS 中，端点是 RDS 实例的主机名
    在 DMS 中，端点是一种资源，其中包含有关数据库类型的信息，还包括主机名和其他连接详细信息

    1. 在上一步中访问的 shell 浏览器窗口中，输入 ls。

    您将看到以下内容：（已创建小型测试数据库文件）

    该数据集包含一个名为 people 的表，该表有六列。

    1. 要将 people.sql 数据加载到源数据库中，请输入以下命令，将 source-mysql-endpoint 替换为您之前从 RDS 记下的端点：

    mysql -P 3306 -u admin -p -h "source-mysql-endpoint" < people.sql

    会要求你输入密码，输入testpass，这个密码我用过。 请注意，密码区分大小写。

    您将看到类似于以下内容的输出：

    该命令有两部分，第一部分是从命令开头到 < 符号。 它告诉 MySQL 命令行客户端创建到源数据库的连接：

    第二部分 < people.sql 使用 Linux Bash shell 的一项称为重定向的功能。 这部分命令将 people.sql 文件的内容输入到 MySQL 客户端的连接中。

  1. 要连接到数据库，请输入以下命令，将 source-mysql-endpoint 替换为您之前从 RDS 检索到的端点：

mysql -P 3306 -u admin -p -h "source-mysql-endpoint" people

系统会要求您输入密码，输入 testpass。

此命令与用于加载 people.sql 文件的命令类似。 这次您的命令不是重定向文件，而是连接到您创建的人员数据库。

将打开 MySQL 客户端命令提示符。

  1. 要验证数据是否已填充，请在 MySQL 命令提示符中输入以下 SQL 查询：

SELECT * FROM people LIMIT 10;

此查询从名为 people 的表中选择前 10 条记录。

您将看到类似于以下内容的输出：

  1. 要查看 people 表中总共有多少行，请在 MySQL 命令提示符中输入以下 SQL 查询：

SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM people;
您将看到以下输出：

  1. 要退出 MySQL 命令提示符并返回到 bash shell，请输入 quit。

创建迁移任务

  1. 导航到 AWS 数据库迁移服务。

  2. 在左侧菜单中，单击数据库迁移任务：

  3. 点击创建任务：

  4. 在“创建数据库迁移任务”表单的“任务配置”部分的“任务标识符”文本框中，输入 test-task：

  5. 为这些下拉字段选择以下值，接受未指定字段的默认值：

复制实例：以 lab-replication-instance 开头的实例 源数据库端点：mysqlsource

目标数据库端点：postgrestarget-1

  1. 在迁移类型下拉列表中，确保选择迁移现有数据：

迁移类型字段允许您指定不同类型的迁移：

KrushiVasani

Posted on Jul 8

AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) is used to transfer data and database applications between different database instances. When migrating data the source and the target databases can use the same database engine, or they can be different engines. The primary use-case for DMS is to enable and support one-time large-scale migration activities.

A secondary use-case is for frequent or long-term replication tasks. Using DMS, a migration process that would previously have involved an outage or a risky and sudden switching of instances can be avoided. Instead, setting up real-time replication across different database instances allows for migration activities to happen more slowly, in smaller steps, and with validation being performed at each stage.

In addition, DMS can also be used for indefinite backup tasks. This is usually more costly than traditional database backup strategies (periodic snapshotting for example), but when the volume of data is very big, or real-time backups are a requirement, DMS is often the most effective and efficient solution.

In this blog we will use DMS to migrate data from a database instance running the MySQL engine to an instance running the Aurora PostgreSQL engine.

Learning Objectives
This is a beginner level blog, upon completion of this blog you will be able to:

  1. Configure source and target endpoints in DMS
  2. Run a migration task in DMS
  3. Connect to MySQL and PostgreSQL databases from the command-line
  4. Generate a pre-migration task assessment in DMS.

Prerequisites
You should have a conceptual understanding of:

  • Amazon RDS
  • SQL and Databases

Steps:

  1. In the AWS Management Console search bar, enter Database Migration Service, and click the Database Migration Service result under Services

  2. In the left-hand menu, click Endpoints.

  3. Click Create endpoint.

  1. In the Endpoint type section of the Create endpoint form, ensure Source endpoint is selected:

  1. Check the Select RDS DB instance checkbox, and in the drop-down box that appears, select mysqlsource: (have created this database in advanced)

This will populate the Endpoint configuration section of the form with values for Server name, Port, and User name.

  1. In the Endpoint configuration section of the form, select Provide access information manually.

  1. In the Password textbox, enter testpass.

By entering the password here you are explicitly giving the Database Migration Service access to the source database.

  1. In the Test endpoint connection section of the form, in the VPC drop-down menu, select the VPC test. (your VPC):

  2. In the Replication instance drop-down, ensure lab-replication-instance is selected:

  3. To create your source endpoint, click Create endpoint:

  4. In the Endpoints list, select the mysqlsource endpoint:

  5. To open the Test endpoint connection form, click Actions, and Test connection:

  6. To test the source endpoint connection, click Run test:

The test will take up to a minute to complete.

Once complete you will see Status field change to successful:

If you don't see a successful connection, it is likely that the password stored in the endpoint is incorrect. Use the management console to modify it and re-enter the password.

Connecting to the Virtual Machine using EC2 Instance Connect

  1. In the AWS Management Console search bar, enter EC2, and click the EC2 result under Services:

  2. To see available instances, click Instances in the left-hand menu:


(will use this instance console later in this blog)

Populating the Source Database

Steps

  1. In the AWS Management Console search bar, enter RDS, and click the RDS result under Services:

  1. In the left-hand side menu, click Databases:

  2. In the list of databases, select mysqlsource:

  1. Under the Connectivity & security heading, make a note of the Endpoint, it will be similar to the following:

You will use this endpoint in a moment to connect to the database from the Linux host.

To avoid confusion, an endpoint resource in DMS is not the same as an endpoint in RDS:

In RDS an endpoint is the hostname of the RDS instance
In DMS an endpoint is a resource that contains information about the type of database and also includes the hostname, and other connection details

  1. In the shell browser window you accessed in the previous Step, enter ls.

You will see the following: (have created small test database files)

people.sql is a file containing sample data to be loaded into the source database. The dataset contains one table called people that has six columns.

  1. To load the people.sql data into the source database, enter the following command, replacing source-mysql-endpoint with the endpoint you noted down earlier from RDS:

mysql -P 3306 -u admin -p -h "source-mysql-endpoint" < people.sql

You will be asked to enter a password, enter testpass, this password i have used. Please note that the password is case-sensitive.

You will see output similar to the following:

This command has two parts, the first part is from the start of the command up to the < symbol. It tells the MySQL command-line client to create a connection to the source database:

-P 3306 specifies the port to connect to -p specifies that a password is required -u admin specifies the username to use when connecting -h source-mysql-endpoint specifies the hostname to connect to

The second part, < people.sql, uses a feature of the Linux Bash shell called redirection. This part of the command is feeding the contents of the people.sql file into the MySQL client's connection.

  1. To connect to the database enter the following command, replacing source-mysql-endpoint with the endpoint you retrieved from RDS previously:

mysql -P 3306 -u admin -p -h "source-mysql-endpoint" people

You will be asked for a password, enter testpass.

This command is similar to the one you used to load the people.sql file. This time your command is not redirecting a file, instead, it is connecting to the people database you created.

A MySQL client command prompt will open.

  1. To verify the data has been populated, enter the following SQL query in the MySQL command prompt:

SELECT * FROM people LIMIT 10;

This query selects the first 10 records from a table called people.

You will see output similar to the following:

  1. To see how many rows there are in total in the people table, enter the following SQL query into the MySQL command prompt:

SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM people;
You will see the following output:

  1. To exit the MySQL command prompt and return to the bash shell, enter quit.

Creating the Migration Task

  1. Navigate to the AWS Database Migration Service.

  2. In the left-hand menu, click Database migration tasks:

  3. Click Create task:

  4. In the Task configuration section of the Create database migration task form, in the Task identifier textbox, enter test-task:

  5. Select the following values for these drop-down fields, accepting the defaults for fields not specified:

Replication instance: Instance beginning with lab-replication-instance Source database endpoint: mysqlsource

Target database endpoint: postgrestarget-1

  1. In the Migration type drop-down, ensure Migrate existing data is selected:

The Migration type field allows you to specify different kinds of migration:

Migrate existing data: This type is the simplest, it migrates data from the source to the target and finishes. Using this type will usually require an outage for the duration of the migration. Migrate existing data and replicate ongoing changes: This type will capture changes to the source during the migration and apply them. With this type outages can be minimized or avoided.

Replicate data changes only: This type assumes you have already performed an initial migration of data from source to target, and want to migrate changes in the source that have occurred since. This allows for more complex migration scenarios, such as the initial migration happening outside of the AWS Database Migration Service.

  1. Scroll down to the Table mappings section of the form, and click Add new selection rule:

Selection rules allow specifying which parts of a database to export. You can create include and exclude rules.

As an example where you would use this, imagine you have a group of one or more database tables that don't have relationships with other tables. Selection rules can be configured to migrate those tables separately from the rest of the database, enabling you to migrate your database in parts. This approach may be less risky and easier to manage than migrating the entire database in one task.

  1. In the Schema drop-down, select Enter schema:

  2. In the Source name field, enter people:

  1. In the Source Table name field, enter people:

You have specified an include selection rule that will migrate the people table from the people database schema.

  1. Scroll down to the Premigration assessment section, and check Enable premigration assessment run:

  2. Under Assessments to run, leave the options at their defaults.

  3. Under Assessment report storage, click Browse S3:
    An S3 bucket choose dialog will open.

  4. Select the bucket by clicking the radio button:

You have to selecte a bucket where the pre-migration task assessment data will be stored.

  1. Under IAM role, select the role called s3-access-for-tasks:

This role should allows DMS to access S3.

  1. To create your migration task, at the bottom of the page, click Create task:

  2. To start your task, in the top right, click Actions and click Restart/Resume.

Items.Title: 设计模式-工厂 | Design Pattern - Factory
Items.Description:
工厂方法设计模式是著名的GoF设计模式之一。 它是一种创建型设计模式，它使用工厂方法来处理创建实例，而无需指定将创建的对象的确切类。

工厂方法是一种创建型设计模式，它定义用于创建对象的接口或抽象类，但允许子类决定将实例化的对象的类型。

该模式被命名为工厂，因为它负责制造对象。 工厂设计模式基于面向对象的封装思想。 一般来说，对象创建代码是在程序的客户端编写的，但是通过工厂设计，我们将对象创建代码封装在工厂方法中。 因此，工厂可以根据提供给它的数据返回程序中提到的各种类之一的对象。

动机

让我们看一个场景，需要创建具有不同配置的不同类型的对象，但所需对象的确切类型可能要到运行时才能知道。

就计算机游戏而言，游戏可能需要创建具有不同能力和特征的不同类型的角色。 然而，所需角色的确切类型可能要到运行时才能知道，因为它可能取决于玩家在游戏中的进度或他们当前正在玩的级别等因素。

如果我们直接使用具体类为每种字符类型创建对象，我们最终会出现代码重复和紧密耦合的代码。 随着字符类型数量的增加，管理创建逻辑变得麻烦且容易出错。 此外，修改或添加新的字符类型需要修改现有的客户端代码，违反了开闭原则。

为了应对这些挑战，我们可以应用工厂设计模式。 工厂设计模式提供了一种结构化的对象创建方法，将创建过程封装在工厂类中，并促进客户端代码和具体类之间的松散耦合。

让我们看下面这个不使用工厂设计模式的C++示例并分析潜在的问题。

 #include <iostream> #include <string> // Character base class class Character { public: virtual void displayInfo() const = 0; virtual void attack() const = 0; virtual void defend() const = 0; virtual ~Character() {} }; // Concrete character classes class Warrior : public Character { public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Warrior: A strong melee fighter with high stamina." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Warrior attacks with a mighty swing!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Warrior raises a sturdy shield to block incoming attacks!" << std::endl; } }; class Mage : public Character { public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Mage: A magical spellcaster with powerful elemental abilities." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Mage casts a powerful fireball!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Mage conjures a protective barrier to deflect enemy spells!" << std::endl; } }; class Archer : public Character { public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Archer: A skilled marksman with exceptional ranged attacks." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Archer fires a precise arrow at the target!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Archer swiftly evades incoming attacks with precise movements!" << std::endl; } }; // Client code int main() { Character *warrior = new Warrior(); Character *mage = new Mage(); Character *archer = new Archer(); warrior->displayInfo(); warrior->attack(); warrior->defend(); mage->displayInfo(); mage->attack(); mage->defend(); archer->displayInfo(); archer->attack(); archer->defend(); delete warrior; delete mage; delete archer; return 0; }

在此示例中，角色是直接在客户端代码中创建的，而不使用工厂设计模式。 每个角色类型（Ex、 Warrior 、 Mage 、 Archer ）都使用具体的类构造函数进行实例化。 成员函数模拟每种角色类型在游戏过程中可以执行的特定操作。

例子中的问题

潜在的问题包括：

  1. 代码重复：如果没有工厂设计模式，我们最终会在整个客户端代码的多个位置重复创建字符的代码。 这可能会导致不一致，并使管理和修改代码库变得更加困难。
  2. 缺乏抽象：客户端代码和角色对象的创建之间没有明确的抽象或分离。 这可能会使代码更难理解、维护和扩展，尤其是随着字符类型数量的增加。
  3. 紧密耦合：客户端代码与具体字符类紧密耦合。 它直接使用对象的构造函数创建对象，这使得在不修改客户端代码的情况下引入新的字符类型或修改创建过程变得更加困难。
  4. 灵活性和扩展性有限：添加新的字符类型或修改创建过程需要修改客户端代码。 这违反了开放关闭原则，该原则规定代码应该对扩展开放，但对修改关闭。

通过使用工厂设计模式，我们可以通过将创建逻辑封装在工厂类中、促进松散耦合、提供抽象层以及在不修改客户端代码的情况下促进可扩展性来解决这些问题。

解决方案

工厂方法模式建议用专用工厂方法的调用来代替直接对象构造调用（使用new运算符进行）。 这在客户端代码和对象的实际创建之间提供了一定程度的间接性。

 #include <iostream> #include <string> // Character base class class Character { public: virtual void displayInfo() const = 0; virtual void attack() const = 0; virtual void defend() const = 0; virtual ~Character() {} }; // Concrete character classes class Warrior : public Character { public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Warrior: A strong melee fighter with high stamina." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Warrior attacks with a mighty swing!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Warrior raises a sturdy shield to block incoming attacks!" << std::endl; } }; class Mage : public Character { public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Mage: A magical spellcaster with powerful elemental abilities." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Mage casts a powerful fireball!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Mage conjures a protective barrier to deflect enemy spells!" << std::endl; } }; class Archer : public Character { public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Archer: A skilled marksman with exceptional ranged attacks." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Archer fires a precise arrow at the target!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Archer swiftly evades incoming attacks with precise movements!" << std::endl; } }; // CharacterFactory class class CharacterFactory { public: static Character *createCharacter(const std::string &characterType) { if (characterType == "Warrior") { return new Warrior(); } else if (characterType == "Mage") { return new Mage(); } else if (characterType == "Archer") { return new Archer(); } else { std::cout << "Invalid character type. Creating a default character." << std::endl; return new Warrior(); // Default to a Warrior character } } }; // Client code int main() { // Create a Warrior character Character *warrior = CharacterFactory::createCharacter("Warrior"); warrior->displayInfo(); warrior->attack(); warrior->defend(); // Create a Mage character Character *mage = CharacterFactory::createCharacter("Mage"); mage->displayInfo(); mage->attack(); mage->defend(); // Create an Archer character Character *archer = CharacterFactory::createCharacter("Archer"); archer->displayInfo(); archer->attack(); archer->defend(); // Clean up the memory delete warrior; delete mage; delete archer; return 0; }

此代码演示了如何使用工厂设计模式根据提供的角色类型创建不同类型的角色（ Warrior 、 Mage 、 Archer ）。 工厂中的createCharacter静态方法采用字符类型作为输入，并返回相应具体字符类的动态分配实例。

在客户端代码中，使用CharacterFactory::createCharacter方法创建各种角色对象，然后通过调用各自的成员函数（ displayInfo 、 attack 、 defend ）来使用它们。 最后删除动态分配的角色对象以释放内存。

下面是表示示例代码的 UML 类图：

在此 UML 类图中：

  • 它有一个createCharacter方法，该方法返回指向创建的字符对象的Character*指针。
  • 客户端代码要求CharacterFactory创建Warrior 、 Mage和Archer的对象。

这种方法有几个优点。

  1. 客户端代码不是将客户端代码紧密耦合到特定的具体类，而是依赖工厂方法来创建对象。 这促进了松散耦合并增强了系统的灵活性。
  2. 工厂方法封装了对象创建过程，允许对象实例化方式的潜在更改或变化。 通过将创建逻辑集中在工厂方法中，可以更轻松地修改或扩展对象创建过程，而不会影响客户端代码。
  3. 工厂方法充当创建对象的单一入口点，允许在创建过程中进行集中控制和一致性。 这在对象创建期间需要额外步骤或验证的情况下特别有用。
  4. 通过工厂方法创建的对象通常称为产品。 这种区别有助于强调这样一个事实：工厂方法负责创建特定类型的对象，并为客户端交互提供清晰的抽象。

如何实施？

  1. 确定公共接口：确定所有产品类将实现的公共接口。 该接口定义了可以对工厂创建的对象执行的操作。

  2. 创建抽象基类：声明一个抽象基类（或接口），表示上一步中标识的公共接口。 该类应该具有定义公共操作的纯虚方法。

  3. 定义具体类：创建从抽象基类继承的具体类。 每个具体类代表可以由工厂创建的特定产品。 在这些具体类中实现基类的虚拟方法。

  4. 实现工厂类：创建一个工厂类，负责创建具体类的对象。 工厂类应该有一个方法，该方法接受参数（例如类型或配置）并返回指向基类（抽象类型）的指针。 在此方法中，使用条件语句或其他逻辑来确定要实例化哪个具体类。

  5. 利用工厂类：在客户端代码中，使用工厂类通过调用适当的工厂方法来创建对象。 客户端代码应该使用抽象基类和工厂方法返回的对象，而不需要知道具体的具体类。

  6. 测试和迭代：测试实现以确保正确创建对象并且工厂按预期运行。 根据您的应用程序的具体要求进行必要的调整或增强。

结论

工厂设计模式提供了一种创建对象的方法，而无需预先指定其确切类型。 通过使用工厂类，您可以集中对象创建、增强代码模块化并方便运行时确定对象类型。 该模式对于对象创建涉及复杂逻辑、不同依赖性或需要节省系统资源的场景非常有价值。 总体而言，工厂设计模式提高了代码灵活性、可维护性和可扩展性。



原文:

The factory method design pattern is one of the well-known GoF design patterns. It is a creational design pattern that uses factory methods to deal with creating instances without specifying the exact class of the object that will be created.

Factory Method is a creational design pattern that defines an interface or abstract class for creating an object, but allows subclasses to decide the type of objects that will be instantiated.

This pattern is named as a factory as it is responsible for manufacturing an Object. Factory design pattern is predicated upon an object-oriented encapsulation idea. In general, Object creation code is written on client side of the program, but with the factory design, we encapsulate object creation code inside the factory method. Therefore, the factory may return an object of one of the various classes mentioned within a program, based on the data supplied to it.

Motivation

Let's look at a scenario where there is a need to create different types of objects with varying configurations, but the exact type of object needed may not be known until runtime.

In the case of a computer game, the game may need to create different types of characters with varying abilities and characteristics. However, the exact type of character needed may not be known until runtime, as it may depend on factors such as the player's progress in the game or the level they are currently playing.

If we were to directly create objects using concrete classes for each character type, we would end up with code duplication and tightly coupled code. As the number of character types increases, managing the creation logic becomes cumbersome and error-prone. Additionally, modifying or adding new character types would require modifying existing client code, violating the principle of Open-Closed principle.

To address these challenges, we can apply the Factory Design Pattern. The Factory Design Pattern provides a structured approach to object creation, encapsulating the creation process within a factory class and promoting loose coupling between the client code and the concrete classes.

!Factory Design Pattern

Let's look at the below C++ example that doesn't use factory design pattern and analyze the potential problems.

#include <iostream>
#include <string>

// Character base class
class Character
{
public: virtual void displayInfo() const = 0; virtual void attack() const = 0; virtual void defend() const = 0; virtual ~Character() {}
}; // Concrete character classes
class Warrior : public Character
{
public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Warrior: A strong melee fighter with high stamina." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Warrior attacks with a mighty swing!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Warrior raises a sturdy shield to block incoming attacks!" << std::endl; }
}; class Mage : public Character
{
public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Mage: A magical spellcaster with powerful elemental abilities." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Mage casts a powerful fireball!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Mage conjures a protective barrier to deflect enemy spells!" << std::endl; }
}; class Archer : public Character
{
public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Archer: A skilled marksman with exceptional ranged attacks." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Archer fires a precise arrow at the target!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Archer swiftly evades incoming attacks with precise movements!" << std::endl; }
}; // Client code
int main()
{ Character *warrior = new Warrior(); Character *mage = new Mage(); Character *archer = new Archer(); warrior->displayInfo(); warrior->attack(); warrior->defend(); mage->displayInfo(); mage->attack(); mage->defend(); archer->displayInfo(); archer->attack(); archer->defend(); delete warrior; delete mage; delete archer; return 0;
}

In this example, characters are created directly in the client code without using the Factory Design Pattern. Each character type (Ex, Warrior, Mage, Archer) is instantiated using the concrete class constructors. The member functions simulate the specific actions that each character type can perform during the game.

Problems in the example

The potential problems include:

  1. Code Duplication: Without the Factory Design Pattern, we end up duplicating the code for creating characters in multiple places throughout the client code. This can lead to inconsistencies and makes it harder to manage and modify the codebase.
  2. Lack of Abstraction: There is no clear abstraction or separation between the client code and the creation of character objects. This can make the code harder to understand, maintain, and extend, especially as the number of character types grows.
  3. Tight Coupling: The client code is tightly coupled to the concrete character classes. It directly creates objects using their constructors, making it harder to introduce new character types or modify the creation process without modifying the client code.
  4. Limited Flexibility and Extensibility: Adding new character types or modifying the creation process requires modifying the client code. This violates the Open-Closed Principle, which states that code should be open for extension but closed for modification.

By using the Factory Design Pattern, we can address these problems by encapsulating the creation logic within a factory class, promoting loose coupling, providing an abstraction layer, and facilitating extensibility without modifying the client code.

The Solution

The Factory Method pattern suggests substituting direct object construction calls (made with the new operator) with invocations of a dedicated factory method. This provides a level of indirection between the client code and the actual creation of objects.

#include <iostream>
#include <string>

// Character base class
class Character
{
public: virtual void displayInfo() const = 0; virtual void attack() const = 0; virtual void defend() const = 0; virtual ~Character() {}
}; // Concrete character classes
class Warrior : public Character
{
public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Warrior: A strong melee fighter with high stamina." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Warrior attacks with a mighty swing!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Warrior raises a sturdy shield to block incoming attacks!" << std::endl; }
}; class Mage : public Character
{
public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Mage: A magical spellcaster with powerful elemental abilities." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Mage casts a powerful fireball!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Mage conjures a protective barrier to deflect enemy spells!" << std::endl; }
}; class Archer : public Character
{
public: void displayInfo() const override { std::cout << "Archer: A skilled marksman with exceptional ranged attacks." << std::endl; } void attack() const override { std::cout << "Archer fires a precise arrow at the target!" << std::endl; } void defend() const override { std::cout << "Archer swiftly evades incoming attacks with precise movements!" << std::endl; }
}; // CharacterFactory class
class CharacterFactory
{
public: static Character *createCharacter(const std::string &characterType) { if (characterType == "Warrior") { return new Warrior(); } else if (characterType == "Mage") { return new Mage(); } else if (characterType == "Archer") { return new Archer(); } else { std::cout << "Invalid character type. Creating a default character." << std::endl; return new Warrior(); // Default to a Warrior character } }
}; // Client code
int main()
{ // Create a Warrior character Character *warrior = CharacterFactory::createCharacter("Warrior"); warrior->displayInfo(); warrior->attack(); warrior->defend(); // Create a Mage character Character *mage = CharacterFactory::createCharacter("Mage"); mage->displayInfo(); mage->attack(); mage->defend(); // Create an Archer character Character *archer = CharacterFactory::createCharacter("Archer"); archer->displayInfo(); archer->attack(); archer->defend(); // Clean up the memory delete warrior; delete mage; delete archer; return 0;
}

This code demonstrates the use of the Factory Design Pattern to create different types of characters (Warrior, Mage, Archer) based on the character type provided. The CharacterFactory class acts as the factory responsible for creating character objects. The createCharacter static method in the factory takes a character type as input and returns a dynamically allocated instance of the corresponding concrete character class.

In the client code, various character objects are created using the CharacterFactory::createCharacter method and then utilized by calling their respective member functions (displayInfo, attack, defend). Finally, the dynamically allocated character objects are deleted to release the memory.

Here is a UML class diagram representing the example code:

In this UML class diagram:

  • The Character class is the abstract base class defining the common interface for all character types.
  • The Warrior, Mage, and Archer classes are concrete classes that inherit from Character and provide specific implementations of the character behaviors.
  • The CharacterFactory class is responsible for creating character objects. It has a createCharacter method that returns a Character* pointer to the created character object.
  • The client code asks CharacterFactory to create an object of Warrior, Mage, and Archer.

This approach offers several advantages.

  1. Instead of tightly coupling the client code to specific concrete classes, the client code relies on the factory method to create the objects. This promotes loose coupling and enhances flexibility in the system.
  2. The factory method encapsulates the object creation process, allowing for potential changes or variations in the way objects are instantiated. By centralizing the creation logic within the factory method, it becomes easier to modify or extend the object creation process without impacting the client code.
  3. The factory method serves as a single entry point for creating objects, allowing for centralized control and consistency in the creation process. This can be particularly beneficial in scenarios where additional steps or validations are required during object creation.
  4. The objects created by the factory method are commonly referred to as products. This distinction helps to highlight the fact that the factory method is responsible for creating specific types of objects and provides a clear abstraction for clients to interact with.

How to implement?

  1. Identify the common interface: Determine the common interface that all the product classes will implement. This interface defines the operations that can be performed on the objects created by the factory.

  2. Create an abstract base class: Declare an abstract base class (or interface) representing the common interface identified in the previous step. This class should have pure virtual methods that define the common operations.

  3. Define concrete classes: Create concrete classes that inherit from the abstract base class. Each concrete class represents a specific product that can be created by the factory. Implement the virtual methods of the base class in these concrete classes.

  4. Implement the factory class: Create a factory class that is responsible for creating objects of the concrete classes. The factory class should have a method that takes in parameters (such as a type or configuration) and returns a pointer to the base class (the abstract type). Within this method, use conditional statements or other logic to determine which concrete class to instantiate.

  5. Utilize the factory class: In the client code, use the factory class to create objects by invoking the appropriate factory method. The client code should work with the abstract base class and the objects returned by the factory method, without being aware of the specific concrete classes.

  6. Test and iterate: Test the implementation to ensure that objects are being created correctly and that the factory is functioning as expected. Make any necessary adjustments or enhancements based on the specific requirements of your application.

Conclusion

The Factory Design Pattern provides a way to create objects without specifying their exact types upfront. By using a factory class, you can centralize object creation, enhance code modularity, and facilitate runtime determination of object types. The pattern is valuable for scenarios where object creation involves complex logic, varying dependencies, or the need to conserve system resources. Overall, the Factory Design Pattern improves code flexibility, maintainability, and scalability.

Items.Title: ⚡️了解React的节点协调和React Fiber的好处⚡️ | ⚡️ Understanding React's Node Reconciliation and the Benefits of React Fiber ⚡️
Items.Description:
随着新的协调引擎 React Fiber 的推出，React 在性能、响应能力和并发渲染方面取得了显着的改进。 在本文中，我们将深入研究 React 如何识别哪些节点需要重新渲染，探索协调算法，并了解 React Fiber 带来的进步。 虚拟 DOM 是真实 DOM 的轻量级内存表示，它允许 React 更改 UI，而无需操作实际 DOM。 这使得更新速度更快，因为更改虚拟 DOM 比更改真实 DOM 更便宜。

当组件的状态或属性发生变化时，React 会将新的 Virtual DOM 与前一个虚拟 DOM 进行比较，以有效地识别差异，从而最大限度地减少不必要的更新并提高性能。 协调算法：

该算法遵循自上而下、深度优先的方法，比较每个节点及其子节点。 让我们了解一下所涉及的关键步骤：🌳

它确定根节点类型是否相同并仅更新已更改的属性。 然后，它继续递归地比较这些组件的子组件，继续比较过程。 它使用一种称为keyed reconciliation技术，其中每个元素都分配有一个唯一的键。 如果元素具有相同的键，React 会假定它们具有相同的类型，并且可以重新排序而不是完全重新渲染。 它使用各种策略，例如“最短路径”算法，来有效地更新 DOM。 识别更改后，React 将多个更改批处理为单个更新，从而减少对虚拟 DOM 的更新次数，进而减少对真实 DOM 的更新次数。 反应纤维🧶：

树中的每个 Fiber 代表需要执行的一个工作单元，例如更新组件或渲染新元素。

当组件的状态发生变化时，React 会为该组件创建一个新的 Fiber 并将其添加到 Fiber 树中。 然后 React 安排 Fiber 进行处理，将其添加到需要处理的 Fiber 队列中。 然后 React 开始处理队列，一次处理一个 Fiber。

这使浏览器能够在大型更新期间响应用户输入，并防止应用程序变得无响应。 这对于严重依赖动画的应用程序（例如游戏或视频播放器）尤其重要。 通过将渲染工作分解为更小的独立单元，React 可以更轻松地利用新的浏览器 API，例如允许并发执行的 Web Workers。🔄

由于React可以随时暂停和恢复工作，因此它可以将渲染过程分割成更小的块，这些块可以在服务器上执行并根据需要发送到客户端。 结论：

通过有效地识别要重新渲染的节点，React 最大限度地减少了不必要的更新并确保最佳性能。 随着 React 的不断发展，开发人员可以期待渲染性能和响应能力方面取得更多进步。 🌈 🌈



原文:

React, a popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces, utilizes a process called "reconciliation" to efficiently update the DOM when there are changes in the application's state.

React's reconciliation algorithm analyzes the virtual representation of the UI and determines which nodes need to be re-rendered. With the introduction of React Fiber, a new reconciliation engine, React has made significant improvements in performance, responsiveness, and concurrent rendering. In this article, we will delve into how React identifies which nodes to re-render, explore the reconciliation algorithm, and understand the advancements brought about by React Fiber. 🚀

React's Virtual DOM:

React operates on a virtual representation of the DOM called the Virtual DOM. The virtual DOM is a lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM, which allows React to make changes to the UI without manipulating the actual DOM. This makes updates faster, as changing the virtual DOM is less expensive than changing the real DOM.

When a component's state or props change, React compares the new Virtual DOM with the previous one to identify the differences efficiently, minimizing unnecessary updates and improving performance. ⚛️

Reconciliation Algorithm:

React's reconciliation algorithm, also known as the diffing algorithm, is responsible for determining the minimal set of changes required to update the real DOM. The algorithm follows a top-down, depth-first approach, comparing each node and its children. Let's understand the key steps involved: 🌳

1. Tree Diffing: React starts by diffing the new Virtual DOM with the previous one, comparing the root nodes. It determines if the root node types are the same and updates only the attributes that have changed. 🌱

2. Component Instances: If the root nodes differ, React creates new instances of the components involved. It then proceeds to recursively diff the children of these components, continuing the comparison process. 🔄

3. Element Reordering: React attempts to minimize changes by reordering elements instead of re-rendering them. It uses a technique called keyed reconciliation where each element is assigned a unique key. If elements have the same keys, React assumes they are of the same type and can be reordered instead of completely re-rendered. 🔑

4. Updating Children: React compares the children of the elements and performs updates based on their changes. It uses various strategies, such as the "shortest path" algorithm, to efficiently update the DOM. After identifying change, React batches multiple changes into a single update, reducing the number of updates to the virtual DOM and, in turn, the real DOM. 🔄

React Fiber 🧶:

React Fiber is a reimplementation of the reconciliation algorithm introduced in React 16. It is designed to improve performance and enable new features like suspense and concurrent rendering. Fiber works by breaking down the reconciliation process into smaller units of work, allowing React to prioritize and interrupt work if needed, resulting in a more responsive user interface. ⚡️

The Fiber tree represents the virtual DOM that allows React to keep track of the work that needs to be done in a more fine-grained way. Each Fiber in the tree represents a unit of work that needs to be performed, such as updating a component or rendering a new element.

When a component’s state changes, React creates a new Fiber for that component and adds it to the Fiber tree. React then schedules the Fiber for processing, adding it to a queue of Fibers that need to be worked on. React then begins processing the queue, one Fiber at a time.

The key to the Fiber architecture is that it allows React to pause and resume work anytime.

The main advantages of React Fiber include:

1. Incremental Rendering: Fiber allows React to split rendering work into chunks or "fibers," which can be interrupted and resumed. This enables the browser to respond to user input during large updates and prevents the application from becoming unresponsive. ⏳

2. Smoother Animations: By allowing React to prioritize animation updates over other work, the Fiber architecture can help to make animations smoother and more responsive. This can be especially important in applications that rely heavily on animations, such as games or video players. 🎯

3. Better Concurrency Support: Lays the groundwork for better concurrency support in React. By breaking up the work of rendering into smaller, independent units, React can more easily take advantage of new browser APIs, such as Web Workers, that allow for concurrent execution.🔄

4. Easier Server-Side Rendering: The Fiber architecture makes it easier to perform server-side rendering in React. Because React can pause and resume work at any time, it can split the rendering process into smaller chunks that can be executed on the server and sent to the client as needed. 🌐

Conclusion:

React's reconciliation algorithm and the introduction of React Fiber have revolutionized the way UI updates are handled in React applications. By efficiently identifying which nodes to re-render, React minimizes unnecessary updates and ensures optimal performance. The introduction of React Fiber further enhances this process, making React more responsive, enabling concurrent rendering, and improving the user experience. As React continues to evolve, developers can expect even more advancements in rendering performance and responsiveness. 🌈

Items.Title: 快速指南，在 Linux 中为用户创建 ssh 密钥对 | Fast guide, creating a ssh key pair for the user in Linux
Items.Description:
聪明的少年

发表于 7 月 8 日

对于这个非常简短的教程，您需要在 Linux 上安装 ssh 客户端和服务器。 要在 Ubuntu 上安装：

$ sudo apt install openssh-client
$ sudo apt install openssh-server

在拱门上

入门

要创建密钥对，请运行以下命令：
$ ssh-keygen -o -a 100 -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -C "{your_email}"

之后将出现一条消息，要求输入如下密码：
Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase):

您只需按 Enter 或写下密码并确认即可。

要获取您的公钥，请输入：
$ cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

现在，您可以复制您的公钥并在您想要的服务的 ssh 客户端上注册，例如 Github 或 Amazon。

所以你不必一直输入密码，你的机器需要有 ssh-agent 服务。 您可以使用以下命令检查是否正在运行：
$ eval(ssh-agent -s)
该命令将返回进程的代理 pid。

如果它正在运行，您可以将新密钥添加到代理，只需键入：
然后它只会询问您一次密码。

做得好！ 现在您是我们社会中可通过密码识别的公民。

塞巴斯蒂安·杜克 G - 6 月 28 日

原文:

Clever Júnior

Posted on Jul 8

For this very short tutorial, you need to have installed the ssh client and server on your linux. To install on Ubuntu:

$ sudo apt install openssh-client
$ sudo apt install openssh-server

On Arch

$ yay -S openssh or $ sudo pacman -S openssh

Getting Started

To create your key pair run this command:
$ ssh-keygen -o -a 100 -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -C "{your_email}"

After this will appear a message to enter a passphrase like this:
Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase):

You can just press Enter or write your passphrase and confirm.

To get your pub key type:
$ cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

Now you can copy your pub key and register on the ssh client of the service you want, like Github or Amazon.

So you don't have to type the passphrase all the time, your machine needs to have the ssh-agent service. You check if is running with this command:
$ eval(ssh-agent -s)
The command will return the agent pid of the process.

If it is running, you can add your new key to the agent, just type:
$ ssh-add ~/ssh/id_ed25519.
Then it only will ask for your passphrase once.

Well done! Now you're a cryptographically identifiable citizen in our society.

Sebastián Duque G - Jun 28
